headline: Tool calling and agentic infrastructure led the month, with new local agentic models and production agent deployments anchoring the rotation.
body: Tool calling topped the movers alongside multilingual speech, vector search, and medical AI, while function calling entered as a new entrant. DeepReinforce released Ornith, a family of open agentic coding models from 9B to 397B built on Gemma and Qwen with tool-call blocks, with 35B and 9B variants tuned for local deployment in GGUF, reinforcing the rise in local models. Production agent work surfaced through OpenGov scaling OG Assist across thousands of governments and a Google Cloud breakdown of agent harness components, and AI memory systems improved with a notes-to-context research OS that skips vector and graph databases. On the research side, optimal transport broke out and new entrants including lost in the middle, posterior collapse, parameterization, and initialization appeared.

cited_items.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
5 things every AI agent harness needs
Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
